pages.title: Philosophy feed
lines: - Thomas E. Hill, Jr., professor emeritus of philosophy at the University of North Carolina, Chapel Hill, has died. Professor Hill was well-known for his work in ethics, the history of ethics, and political philosophy, and especially for his scholarship on Kantian moral and political philosophy. He is the author of Beyond Duty: Kantian Ideals of Respect, Beneficence, and Appreciation (2022), Virtue, Rules, and Justice: Kantian Aspirations (2012), Human Welfare and Moral Worth: Kantian Perspectives
- [A Scene from the AI Flooding of Academic Journals](https://dailynous.com/2026/07/20/a-scene-from-the-ai-flooding-of-academic-journals/) — 2026-07-20
- [Four Philosophy Professors Among New British Academy Fellows](https://dailynous.com/2026/07/20/four-philosophy-professors-among-new-british-academy-fellows/) — 2026-07-20

pages.title: Weekly sit-down
lines: :root{color-scheme:light;--paper:#f6f1e8;--ink:#1c1b19;--muted:#6c665d;--line:#d9d0c2;--accent:#a54832;--soft:#eee6d9}*{box-sizing:border-box}body{margin:0;background:var(--paper);color:var(--ink);font-family:Georgia,'Times New Roman',serif;line-height:1.55}.page{max-width:76rem;margin:auto;padding:1.4rem 1.15rem 3rem}a{color:inherit}.mast{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;border-bottom:2px solid var(--ink);padding-bottom:.35rem;font:700 .68rem Arial,sans-serif;letter-spacing:.16em;text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--muted)}.mast strong{font:700 clamp(1.7rem,4vw,3rem) Georgia,serif;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:none;color:var(--ink)}.hero{display:grid;grid-template-columns:minmax(0,1.6fr) minmax(15rem,1fr);gap:2rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--ink);padding:2rem 0 1.4rem}.eyebrow{font:700 .68rem Arial,sans-serif;letter-spacing:.18em;text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--accent)}h1{font-size:clamp(2.2rem,6vw,5.6rem);line-height:.95;letter-spacing:-.06em;margin:.35rem 0 1rem}.lede{font-size:1.08rem;max-width:42rem;color:#403c35}.meta{border-left:1px solid var(--line);padding-left:1.2rem;font: .78rem Arial,sans-serif;color:var(--muted)}.meta strong{display:block;color:var(--ink);font-size:1.35rem;margin:.2rem 0 .5rem}.stats{display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(4,1fr);border-bottom:1px solid var(--ink);margin:1rem 0 1.3rem}.stat{padding:.75rem .9rem;border-left:1px solid var(--line)}.stat:first-child{border-left:0;padding-left:0}.stat b{display:block;font-size:1.55rem}.stat span{font:700 .62rem Arial,sans-serif;letter-spacing:.12em;text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--muted)}.content{display:grid;grid-template-columns:1.15fr .85fr;gap:1.4rem}.panel{border-top:2px solid var(--ink);padding-top:.45rem;margin-bottom:1.4rem}.panel h2{font:700 .72rem Arial,sans-serif;letter-spacing:.18em;text-transform:uppercase;margin:.2rem 0 .75rem;color:var(--muted)}.panel h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1rem 0 .25rem}.panel p,.panel li{color:#39352f}.panel ul{padding-left:1.2rem;margin:.4rem 0}.panel li{margin:.45rem 0}.event{display:grid;grid-template-columns:8.5rem 1fr;gap:1rem;border-top:1px solid var(--line);padding:.7rem 0}.event:first-child{border-top:0}.label{font:700 .62rem Arial,sans-serif;letter-spacing:.12em;text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--accent)}.event p{margin:0}.qa{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font: .82rem Arial,sans-serif}.qa th{text-align:left;font-size:.62rem;letter-spacing:.12em;text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--muted);border-bottom:1px solid var(--ink);padding:.45rem}.qa td{vertical-align:top;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.65rem .45rem}.notice{background:var(--soft);padding:.75rem;border-left:4px solid var(--accent);font: .8rem Arial,sans-serif}.foot{border-top:2px solid var(--ink);padding-top:.5rem;color:var(--muted);font:.68rem Arial,sans-serif}@media(max-width:800px){.hero,.content{grid-template-columns:1fr}.meta{border-left:0;border-top:1px solid var(--line);padding:1rem 0 0}.stats{grid-template-columns:repeat(2,1fr)}.stat{border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}.stat:nth-child(odd){border-left:0;padding-left:0}.event{grid-template-columns:1fr;gap:.2rem}}@media(max-width:480px){.page{padding:.9rem}.mast{display:block}.mast strong{display:block;margin-bottom:.35rem}.stats{grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr}.stat{padding:.65rem .5rem!important}}
Akira DispatchWeekly review · Virginia Beach · Week ending 2026-07-17

Research, operations, and durable memory

Weekly sit-down

A concise evidence review of what moved, what changed our understanding, what was answered, and what still requires work.

Evidence window11–17 July 2026

Vault notes, derived artifacts, file mtimes, cron/jobs.json, and publisher state.

No Git repository was present at the Akira root or vault in this environment. Commit history and diffs are therefore not part of this report.

7Days covered
58 / 89AI graph entities / relations
73Car leads captured 17 Jul
4 / 71Fall sections / enrollment

1) Accomplished

Research

Ran the AI-agents loop through 17 July and refreshed its weekly briefing and research update. The current graph reports 58 entities, 89 relationships, 414 wikilinks, and 21 findings.

Publishing

Refreshed the public Akira artifact set: daily digest, car search, cyber, philosophy, Techmeme, weather, birthdays, and job status. The digest recorded 73 captured listings and 20 actively exploited vulnerabilities added to CISA KEV in the last 30 days.

Energy

Completed a first Dominion-based solar analysis using a full year of interval data: 15,727.9 kWh annual use, 43.1 kWh/day average, 68.2 kWh/day summer average, and a late-afternoon/evening load concentration.

Teaching

Filed and validated the PHI 220 Summer 2026 Canvas export: 11 modules, 45 items, 14 assignments, 9 discussions, 5 quiz/exam items, and 17 listed files/media. Filed the Fall schedule: four sections and 71 students shown.

Engineering

Added or refreshed tested operational code for cannabis assessment/reporting, car search, eBay search, dispatch templates, and the HTML publisher.

3) Asked / answered

No explicit question-and-answer log was found. These are the decision questions answered by durable artifacts.

QuestionAnswer
Is the AI-agent stream advancing?Yes. Daily and weekly artifacts were refreshed through 17 July, with graph metrics and current control-surface findings recorded.
Is solar worth modeling?Yes, as a comparison, not yet a purchase recommendation. Tariff, export, shading, roof, and spike-load questions remain open.
Is the PHI 220 package safe to preserve?Yes, as an unchanged export plus a separate corrections note. Embedded dates require review.
Should the sit-down create dated pages?No. One stable weekly_sitdown.html is refreshed in place.

2) Learned

  • Agent maturity is increasingly about control surfaces: cost, context quality, memory, search progress, MCP/tool drift, and deployment monitoring.
  • Solar sizing cannot be inferred from annual consumption alone. The east/west roof and evening load make a west-weighted or balanced array worth modeling, but export-credit economics are not established by the public page alone.
  • A Canvas export records state; it does not guarantee coherent dates. The Natural Law date predates its module, and the Final Exam has stale-looking text without a Canvas dueAt.
  • Derived HTML is a publication layer. Durable notes, publisher inclusion, index discovery, and live semantic checks all matter.
  • The research portfolio is bifurcated: several loops have substantive findings; biology, mathematics, statistics, and architecture remain queue-heavy or source-blocked.

4) Open loops

  • Verify the first unattended cron cycle and complete the old-home-quiet migration check.
  • Confirm model fallback on the next ChatGPT Plus 429; watch for Obsidian skill-trigger collisions.
  • Obtain account-specific Dominion terms, model four PV orientations, and explain recurring load spikes before considering batteries.
  • Turn AI-agent findings into a measurable evaluation matrix for cost, safety, memory integrity, context, and tool drift.
  • Process primary sources for the queue-heavy research loops instead of extending scaffold-level claims.
  • Perform browser-level and public semantic verification after this page refresh.

5) Next actions

  • Run the canonical publisher with push and verify the public index, served branch, and cache-busted live page.
  • Keep the Markdown source and stable HTML synchronized next week.
  • Run unattended-cycle checks and close each open item from actual output.
  • Build the solar comparison after tariff terms are pinned down.
  • Convert the AI-agent stream into a small control-plane evaluation matrix.

Cross-thread pattern

Akira is repeatedly moving from raw material to governed decision surfaces: source notes become graphable entities, research claims become tests and caveats, operational jobs become inspectable HTML, and teaching artifacts preserve evidence while exposing anomalies. The common requirement is traceable transformation from evidence to action, not simply more output.
Canonical source: 90-derived/dashboards/weekly_sitdown.md · Stable public page: weekly_sitdown.html · Akira index

pages.title: Agent memory software layer
lines: title: "Agent memory as a software layer"
Agent memory as a software layer

The strongest product direction is a [[50-research/agent-memory-software-layer/entities/Provenance-first Agent Memory|provenance-first agent-memory layer]]: an immutable evidence journal with temporal validity, policy-aware scopes, replaceable derived indexes, and a very small UNIX-friendly interface.

pages.title: Amazon orders
lines: """Derive rough Amazon purchase categories from amazon-orders-pp-cli cache.
Reads the Printing Press Amazon Orders SQLite cache, extracts visible product
link titles from the cached raw HTML, writes reviewable derived tables back into
that same Akira-scoped database, and renders a small self-contained dashboard.
This is intentionally heuristic: the source cache currently has raw Amazon HTML
and item titles, not authoritative Amazon taxonomy or normalized spend fields.
db_path is often on an SMB network mount; smbfs's advisory-lock support is
unreliable, so a freshly-opened connection can transiently fail with
"unable to open database file" even though the file is fine. Retry briefly
before giving up on a real error.
"""Open a read-write sqlite connection, retrying transient SMB lock failures."""

pages.title: Functionalism lectures
lines: - Mind-brain identity theory says mental states are identical to specific brain states.
- That works only if mental states require one physical substrate.
- Functionalism enters because the substrate story looks too narrow.
- Visual: simple fork diagram: dualism vs physicalism vs functionalism.
- A functional kind is defined by what it does, not what it is made of.
- Same job, many possible physical realizations.
- Examples: table, mouse trap, instructor, vending machine.
Slide 3 — The identity theory breaks here
- If pain were identical to one human brain state, other creatures could not feel pain.
- Octopuses are the lecture's pressure test.
- They look different internally but still seem capable of pain.
- Visual: human brain vs octopus brain with the same "pain" label.
Slide 4 — Mental states as causal roles
- Functionalism defines mental states by inputs and outputs.
- A state is mental because of its role in a system.
- Causes become inputs; effects become outputs.
- Visual: input → state → output flow diagram.
- Input: seeing a photo of an ex plus a memory.
- The substrate can vary; the causal pattern does the work.
- Visual: social-media post + memory → emotional reaction loop.
- Functionalism keeps physicalism without tying mind to one material.
- It explains why non-human or artificial systems might count as minded.
- The question shifts from "what is it made of?" to "what role does it play?"
- Visual: conclusion slide with the slogan: "If it can do the job, it can be the state."

pages.title: Cyber 8-K market reaction
lines: Scrapes board-cybersecurity.com's incident tracker for the N most recent
SEC 8-K cybersecurity disclosures (deduped to one row per company), enriches
each with SEC EDGAR metadata (item type 1.05 vs 8.01, acceptance timestamp,
earnings-filing confounder flag), fetches dividend/split-adjusted daily
closes from Yahoo Finance (Stooq fallback, unadjusted), and computes the
price change from the last close before the filing to ~1 week and ~1 month
after — both raw and as excess return vs SPY over the identical window,
with a bootstrap CI and sign test on the medians.
The short backtest enters at the first close actually tradable after the
EDGAR acceptance time (after-hours filings enter next day) and deducts a
Outputs a self-contained HTML report (chart + table) and prints a summary.
HTTP responses are cached under ~/.cache/cyber8k/ (prices/EDGAR 1 day,
ticker map 7 days), so reruns are fast and gentle on the sources.
Note: Yahoo occasionally returns empty history for delisted or
recently-reorganized tickers; affected rows show "no data" in the report
and are counted in the survivorship caveat.
